entry.title: Re: Data Monitor-Reported data Validation rules-Comments
entry.content: As long as the data is stored in database without encryption, you could be able to achieve this. Please explore the tables starting from VEC. The validation is cross components which means also works for FI in addition to Group Reporting.

entry.title: Re: Error No Memory Available in Preparation for Consolidation Group Changes / PCC
entry.content: Total of 24GB memory for a work process is a little bit large. The code line in the dump occurred might not be the root cause, maybe create an incident with SAP to further check.

entry.title: Re: Gropup reporting on premise 2023/SP1 - enable the hie...
entry.content: the master cds view only handle the hierarchy within the same table via HRRP*. So the node is text rather than other dimension such as project -> WBS. In a word, level based hierarchy or external hierarchy ( BW terminology ) is not supported in this case.

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)
entry.content:
I am writing to express my interest in this SAP Digital Skills Initiative Program. I  fall under the category of "People looking to switch their careers mid-life". 

I have gained experience in Logistics, IT, Project Management, Retail and Transport which supports that I am best candidature for this role. I am well disciplined, structured, honest, and energetic individual thriving hard for uphold the values of quality, agility, and professionalism. During this Experience I regularly collaborated with all team members, range of customers to work with strict deadlines, maintain accuracy and quality, effectiveness and minimal risk.

From this experience I have developed an understanding of processes, procedures and business insights, requirements and more. I have developed many skills during my education and experience, and I believe I have abundance of transferable skills that can make me a valuable professional in SAP Digital Industry.

I have attained Masters degree in Manufacturing and Diploma of Project Management. I aim develop as a SAP Consultant using this Digital Skills Initiative Program. 

Hence my request to all relevant stakeholders in this learning journey to provide support and guidance to proceed and succeed. 

 

Thanks 

Satish

entry.title: Re: Employee Central Mass Data Management for Position
entry.content: We have also implemented this tool, keen to also understand if SAP will be adding additional attributes like Parent Position as per the last post.

entry.title: Re: Video Series - Part 10 - Testing CAP application through Postman
entry.content:
Hi @JeyakumarAsai,

This sounds to me as if you would have an issue with your environment variable APP_URL. My first thoughts would be: is your app server up and running? In case answer is Yes, I would review the video where I am showing how to get the right URL (around minute 5:30). 

Hope this gives you some hints.

Regards.

Alejandro.

entry.title: New Member Intro
entry.content:
Hello Everyone,

 

My name is Tiffany and I’m excited to be a part of this SAP Learning Community! A little about myself: I have years of front facing customer service experience, many more years working with computers. I’m very curious and spend a lot of time learning about various things that interest me. I also enjoy socializing, networking and traveling from weekend road trips to abroad.

 

I’m currently working towards my SAP Certified Associate - Implementation Consultant - SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition with the Vower learning group, and I’m really looking forward to expanding my knowledge in supply chain.

 

I’m particularly excited about obtaining this certification because I’m looking to expand beyond IT help desk, and really explore SAP Cloud

 

I’m also open to connecting with others who are interested in cloud and AI solutions for the supply chain industry.

Looking forward to learning and growing with all of you!

 

Best regards,  

Tiffany Christman

entry.title: Re: Hi

entry.title: Re: Hi
entry.content: Hi

entry.title: Re: Hi
entry.content: Am here

entry.title: Re: BRIM-The subscriber account "00000*****" (with "BRIM"...
entry.content: I am getting similar error. Is there any way to fix the issue?

entry.title: How to ppdate payment document with UTR number when processing bank statements
entry.content:
The requirement is to update the payment reference field (KIDNO) with UTR number when processing bank statements.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: How to ppdate payment document with UTR number when p...
entry.content:
The standard process for bank statement processing using the UTR number is as follows:

The supplier invoice will be posted using either "Create Incoming Invoices" or "Create Supplier Invoices - Advanced".

The automatic clearing is then executed, and the Payment Reference field (KIDNO) in the payment document is updated with the UTR number.

In the bank statement format MT940, tag 86 contains the UTR number. The interpretation algorithm to be used is 801 - India Reference Number for Payment Order.

As per the definition of UTR, it can be 6, 16, or 22 characters long and can only consist of alphanumeric characters. Additionally, it should start with the name of the bank, such as HSBC, CITI, HDFC, etc. Please ensure that your UTR meets these criteria.

See KBA 3525585 - "Update payment document with UTR number when processing bank statements" as well.

entry.title: Re: Material Ledger - MLDOC mystery for V/2 materials.
entry.content:
Thank you for the explanation, I am trying to find a way to join MLDOC, MLDOCCCS and ACDOCA. Do you know if it is possible?

Kind Regards

MM

Staying updated with SAP news, tracking my Devtoberfest score, and learning from the community – all in one view!

entry.title: Re: New Phase Model for the Maintenance Processes in S/4HANA Cloud

In the Private cloud system, can customers edit/modify the subphases to meet their requirements

entry.title: Do I need to submit an EWA with my CQC application?
entry.content:
Dear Expert

I would like to know when to submit my PERSON_NAME report in relation to my CQC application.
In “DIGITS Apply for Continuous Quality Check (CQC)” I am applying for CQC. I contacted you because I was asked to do the PERSON_NAME report questions on this incident with the SV-SMG-SER- PERSON_NAME component.

Best Regards


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Do I need to submit an EWA with my CQC application?
entry.content:
Dear customer,

It is not mandatory to submit the report, it is decided by customer.

If you want to setup Self-Service Session, please refer:
2792789 - How to create a Self-Service Session in SAP Solution Manager 7.2 

If you want to SAP Delivery Session, please create case under SV-BO-REQ.

If you want to setup EWA report, please refer:
2282944 - EarlyWatch Alert: Solution Manager 7.2 How to Setup / Configure EWA Reports or Add Email Recipients

If you are facing issue when setup EWA, please refer:
1907383 - How to setup and troubleshoot EarlyWatch Alert reports in SAP Solution Manager
1769513 - EWA not generated - session data is overdue (red flag status)

entry.title: Re: How do you deal with User Custom Fields in Story Reports?
entry.content:
Hello Uwe,

We are also facing the same issue when running a Story report.

It seems that the Story report / People Analytics reports on the Description of Learning Custom Fields, but when UDF runs, this field is not populated. We are left with options to manually maintain each description within the custom field. And some of our customers have hundreds of lines in each custom field and that list is updated on a regular basis.

This could be one of the biggest show stoppers for our customers, not to mention the Story report may also require more than one locale maintained in LMS.

Hopefully SAP can look into this ASAP and provide some easier solution.

Thanks, Sunny

entry.title: Re: SAP Hana error: Number XXX not defined for material type
entry.content:
Hi All,

I've found the assignment in "Define Attributes of Material Types".

Thank you.

entry.title: Always login HANA via Live Connection in SAP Analytics Cloud
entry.content:
My company currently using SAC connect to SAP HANA database (on-premise, not HANA cloud). I want to provide about 50 SAC account for 50 Sale Employee in order to view their sales report. The problem is I use a Live Connection, so I must do Data Authorization in HANA. So for each SAC account there is a corresponding HANA account to authorize. I've tried 2 Direct Live Connection: Username Password and SSO. But both of them need to login HANA again every time I restart my laptop or shut down and power on again (for Username Password there is a Set Credential pop-up when access to the story, and for SSO I need to go <host>:<port> to login the HANA account again. But I don't want to provide my sale employees HANA account, It's very sensitive. I just want to go to their device and login HANA once. The questions are: 
1. Is there another solution for my case beside Username Password and SSO?
2. Is there any way to keep the login of Username Password to HANA in SAC, or keep always login in <host>:<port> for SSO after every time I restart the devices?
Thank you !

Gavan

Felicia Bennett

entry.title: Re: Consuming SAP with SAP Build Apps - Web App in SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition
entry.content:
Hi @egor_t , the foundation plan that is configured by the SAP Build Code booster covers SAP Start, which is bare minimal functionality. However, the SAP Build Apps booster configures the standard plan, which is needed when deploying web apps from SAP Build Apps.

The two boosters are not (yet) aligned on the service plans required for the individual solutions. 

Thanks,
Ludo

entry.title: Error: Manual invoice processing for reference document not permitted
entry.content:
We have created an invoice 123 by using follow up new invoice function. Now we are going to create a follow up credit memo for the invoice 123, but get error: Manual invoice processing for reference document not permitted

How can we make a credit note? What can we do?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Error: Manual invoice processing for reference docume...
entry.content:
Creating a Follow-Up credit memo from an invoice which was created as a follow-up document is not supported. This is the reason for the error message "Manual Invoice Processing for Reference Document not Permitted".

Instead of create credit memo for the invoice, you can cancel the invoice. The invoiced amount will be deducted from the customer balance.

entry.title: Re: Download File from Solution Documentation 7.2 in PDF Format
entry.content: We helped you to accept the answer, since there were no updates after 7 days. You can unaccept it anytime if the answer provided was not helpful enough and you have further questions.

entry.title: Re: Automating SAP DSP Task Chains with CLI Wrapping: FastAPI Integration with SAP Analytics Cloud
entry.content:
Hello @christian_willi 

thanks for the nice blog with the detailed explanations.
BTW I'm also waiting on a official Solution provided by SAP for the problem of triggering DSP Taskchain outside of DSP. (e.g. from SAC, or in the BW at the End of a BW Process Chain via an ABAP Programm(REST API Call), or any other Source System finishing a batch Dataload )

Is the CLI on the Local(User) Machine not also a Limitation or did I miss something and the CLI is only neccessary for the setup? ( but how would the BTP App reach DSP than? ) 
In your Scenario does the DSP have to be in the same BTP as the created BAS-APP? 

Did you check this Blog, there is no CLI required to trigger a DSP Chain (other Usecase than your's):
https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-members/how-to-run-sap-dwc-task-chain-from-sap-btp-cloud-foundry/ba-p/13565558
I think this is the same approach @CasCriel is talking about in his comment, that the CLI is not required because you can trigger it via the DSP API Endpoint.

Kind regards
Julian

entry.title: Re: Moving from Solman to C-ALM, what about users with no...
entry.content:
Dear Customer, 

At the moment, CALM does not have a plan in place to support EWA reports. FRUN does support EWA by sending the content to SAP to generate the reports, therefore it will not function without a connection to SAP. It is recommended to maintain an on-premise Solution Manager to support the EWA report. SAP is aware of customers in this situation and will work towards providing a final solution in the future. Thank you for your understanding and patience.

Best regards,
Vera Jiang

entry.title: Re: Optimizing Data Load for Large Replication Flow Object
entry.content: I would add a Hashtag for S4HANA since the Problem is clearly within the S4HANA System and not the DSP.

entry.title: Re: FSCM - Credit Limit Requests Approval using Manage Credit Management Rules
entry.content:
Hi @SAP_TROJAN ,

Please check the status profile assigned to the case type you are using under Case Management. Then, check the values of the status profile you are using via S_EKW_85000007.

Regards,

Darrel

entry.title: Re: Typing a value doesn't show any value from dropdown i...
entry.content:
To be able to search items in advanced search, or editor area, first you need to define its simple search. Let's say your itemtype has 2 fields in it, code and otherField, and you want those 2 fields to be searchable, you need to define it in backoffice config like this:

<context type="MyItemType" component="simple-search">
	<simple-search:simple-search>
		<simple-search:field name="code" />
		<simple-search:field name="otherField" />
	</simple-search:simple-search>
</context>

entry.title: Re: Always login HANA via Live Connection in SAP Analytics Cloud
entry.content: Looks like the SSO is not correctly configured between HANA and SAC

entry.title: Re: solr admin page connection refused after upgrading to...
entry.content:
This happens because solr only allows access from 127.0.0.1 (its own machine), where your case, you installed it on WSL and tried to access it from Windows, so it counts as remote access.

to resolve this, I usually just edit the bin/solr file (for Linux), and add these lines on top:

SOLR_HOST=0.0.0.0
SOLR_JETTY_HOST=0.0.0.0

 After that, you just need to restart the solr and it will be able to be accessed from Windows.

entry.title: Re: Sales Pricing analysis Fiori App
entry.content:
Yes ,

It is there , please use the app manage sales order and then open the sales order item. Click on the conditions tab as shown in the screen shot.

And then select the Pricing condition type and then click on analysis button .

entry.title: Re: No service provider was defined for CoCd XXXX
entry.content:
E Document Cockpit is used mainly to down load incoming electronic documents in the S/4 Public cloud from other source systems.
Please check if the system connections are proper and also if the data mapping s are correctly maintained in your client.

entry.title: SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud Edition October Bootcamp
entry.content:
Hello Everyone,

 

My name is Brianna Dickey, and I’m excited to be a part of this SAP Learning Community! A little about myself: I’m a boy mom to an amazing 1.5 year old, I enjoy working out and traveling and trying new food dishes. My career background is in Administration, Data Analytics and IT Support.

I’m currently working towards my SAP Certified Associate - Implementation Consultant - SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition with the Vower learning group, and I’m really looking forward to expanding my knowledge in SAP Analytics & more.

 

I’m particularly excited about obtaining this certification because I will be more knowledgeable in this subject and can grow in a blooming field all while being able to work from home and raise my son as a single mom. 

 

I’m also open to connecting with others who are interested in AI, information technology, etc. 

Looking forward to learning and growing with all of you!

 

Best regards,

Brianna Dickey

entry.title: Key Highlights from the 2H 2024 Release of SuccessFactors Onboarding
entry.content:
Hello Community ,

Kindly find 2H2024 Key features for Onboarding Module :

1.GenAI Writing Assistant for Onboarding Module :

    Use case: It will Improve the content for the Welcome Message, Farewell Message, Buddy Messages and Goals content.we can utilize Enhance Writing. With the help of Enhance Writing, you
can enhance the text, change tone, make the text shorter/longer.

    Persona:  Hiring Managers / Admins assigned for NHA Activities .

    Configurations:
             A)enable the AI-Services Administration > Assisted Writing with Text Analyzer option in Admin Center.
             B)RBP:Administrator > Manage AI Capabilities > AI Services Administration permission
                         User Permissions > AI Access > Assisted Writing permission.

2)Concurrent Employment Support for Onboarding Module :

    Use case: Employees who are assigned with one or more future dated concurrent employments can now view and complete all the assigned onboarding tasks.

    Persona:  Employees.

    Configurations:
             A)Configure business rule -Scenario - Trigger Onboarding for Concurrent Employment-create rule.
             B)Map the rule in Admin Center-Manage Data - Onboarding Configuration - DEFAULT_ONB2_CONFIG (DEFAULT_ONB2_CONFIG)-Initiate onb for concurrent employment.
           C)enable the new (ONB) Concurrent Employment User Welcome Message Trigger email template from                the Admin Center > Email Services to send the welcome e-mail notification to
              employees on concurrent employment
           D)RBP:Permission > Onboarding or Offboarding Object Permissions- View permission for Process User                         Task and New Hire Task Summary
                       Assigned user should have target population as external users.

 

3)Offboarding Process variant :

    Use case: You can add a new process variant for offboarding process. It can be now edit, copy, and delete                       the offboarding process flow in the Process Variant Manager.
                     2 document flows can be created in new offboarding flow.   

    Persona:  Offboardee.

    Configurations:
                 A)Configure a business rule using the Select Process Variant rule scenario.
                B)Configure a business rule using the Assign Form rule scenario to add up to two Document Flow                        steps to the same process.

kindly refer WNV article for more details :Offboarding Process Flow Now Available in Process Variant Manager | SAP Help Portal

Thanks,
Gopinath M
Senior Solution Support Architect(Product Area Lead) - Onboarding, Technical Support, India
SAP Labs India Private Limited ,Bangalore.

entry.title: Re: RPA/Bots: Are they really different from....?
entry.content:
There are multiple queries raised in the same thread and so i am going to reply one by one.

Q1. Here, I am discussing specifically about the use-case of employing a RPA/Bot to read from a file and populate data into S/4, because a released API for that functionality isn't available.

Answer: There are multiple apps available in S4 Public cloud provided by Different LOB to read file and then create the related Business object automatically. 

Q2. Are they upgrade safe?
Answer : Yes if you use the standard apps they are upgrade safe.

Q3. So, why is Bot still being considered as an approach this scenario?
Answer: There are build apps as well provided for Customer available for enhancement and achieve further flexibility in case certain requirement does not meet the current standard features. 

Q4. RPA/Bots in this use-case, are they really any different from BDC?

Answer:BDC is old technology and is not having applicability to handle scenarios in Public cloud

entry.title: Re: DMEE XML file missing nodes
entry.content: Solved. The problem was in incorrect condition of a superior node.

entry.title: Re: Profit center template uplode error.
entry.content:
The error states that the file is corrupted. 
Request you to open the migration project and the migration object and check if there is an update required. In case there are new fields added in the system then the template needs to be updated.
Download the updated template and then try again.

entry.title: Re: Flexible Workflow Steps and Approval Logic
entry.content:
Hello Again

Thanks a lot for the link. We have implemented HR completely a few years ago and concepts like personnel administration and organizational data are implemented and currently in use. In addition, all the steps mentioned in the link including maintenance of records of table V_T77S0 and program /SHCM/RH_SYNC_BUPA_EMPL_SINGLE are okay but in transaction SLG1 i get the following errors in log:
HCM001 identification Category Type is missing. (Message No. /SHCM/EMPLOYEE_BL069)
&
HCM001 Address Type is missing. (Message No. /SHCM/EMPLOYEE_BL070)
and still can not assign any user name to a workflow step and get the error "invalid username"
Would you please further guide me on the issue please?
@Bart_Maesen
@ A LL

entry.title: Re: showAdditionalAttributes does not work for SBPA Process UI task
entry.content: Hello Experts any inputs ,feedback on above issue?

entry.title: Re: RAP, create child entities based on static factory ac...
entry.content:
The Dump clearly indicates that there is reference error . Which means you are trying to assign a field using the field symbols which is not existing in the original internal structure of the RAP object. 
Please check the RAP Object Fields and in your code map them to the correct field names of the structure.

entry.title: Re: Right roles for customer user in ITSM for PCOE
entry.content:
Dear Customer, 

Please see the steps in KBA  1817086 - How to assign Business Role and solve message "Logon is not possible because you have not been assigned to a business role".

Best regards,
Vera Jiang

entry.title: Re: Getting 502 error on SAP MDK Web Application
entry.content:
Hi @bill_froelich,

As you suggest we have tried with Cloud Connector SSO, but still issue persist.

entry.title: DMEE xml output BKREF missing
entry.content:
The field LFBK-BKREF (Reference Details for Bank Details) is populated in vendor master. After the payment run is performed, the value is correctly populated in REGUH-BKREF. However, the reference is not passed to the xml output file. The node itself appears in the output but without the reference value. The node mapping is FPAYH-BKREF. When using the test function with parameter DMEE_PAYM_TEST_FPAYH-BKREF, the reference is correctly displayed in the node as intended (test value 000035 - see attached pic). However, in output file the value is blank (i.e. "000000" as of node definition - length 6, conv. func. C0).

What could be the cause? Is the mapping LFBK-BKREF -> FPAYH-BKREF correct?

entry.title: Re: Multiple lines in story report for compensation
entry.content: you can use cross table to get one row for your data

entry.title: Stop delivery distribution
entry.content:
Delivery distribution to be set to stop distribution at delivery level as client wants to make changes after EWM delivery creation as well.


Distribution mode option is not available at config level for us to make the change. 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Stop delivery distribution
entry.content:
Hi,

 

The Customizing field "Distrbtn Mode" is hidden at Customizing "Define Delivery Types" for SAP S/4HANA Cloud System.

You may consider achieve it with BADI LE_SHP_CHECK_DISTRIBUTION - Control Distribution of Delivery Documents.

 

  1. Created Customized field with business context Delivery Header.
  2. Implement the BADI to check if the customized field is initial.
  3. When creating the delivery, it is hold by default. The delivery can be distributed once the customized field is changed according to the custom logic.

 

Attached screenshot.

Best Regard,

entry.title: Re: Error during delivery
entry.content:
In S4 Public Cloud  this kind of error occurs when there is a mismatch between the delivery type and delivery item category assignment.

Please check in the implementation activities in the below nodes.
1, Define Delivery Types  having your delivery type AF
2. Define delivery item categories having the item category type TAN
3.Define Item Category Determination in Deliveries. having the right item category group and item category assigned to the right delivery type.

entry.title: Re: Key Highlights from the 2H 2024 Release of SuccessFactors Onboarding
entry.content:
4. Joule for SAP Onboarding :

    Use case:

Enable Onboarding Manager Tasks Completion via Joule​

Assist New Hire during Onboarding via Joule​

Enable Onboarding Task Completion via Joule - update API​

Answer Policy Questions of New Hire during Onboarding with SAP Joule

     Persona: Hiring Manager and Onboardee

 

1)HM Tasks : Just type "Show my open tasks " and it will show all open tasks assigned for                        manager. It will navigate task in another tab so that managers can complete activity.       

 2)Assign a Buddy Task (Transactional ) : Quicker assignment of Buddy to new hires using joule

Meshii

entry.title: Re: 🟡 Techtoberfest 2024
Re: Error message: material is not maintained when creati...
entry.content:
This is purely customizing issues.
Please check if the pricing procedures are maintained in the previous order document types.
And also there are combination of applicable billing types.
Only if the applicability of the billing item lines maintained in the order document matches then only billing document can be created.

entry.title: Re: Seamless Integration: Connecting Sales Cloud V2 Opportunity Management with S/4HANA Customer Pro
entry.content:
Hi ,

Integrating SAP Sales Cloud V2 with S/4HANA Customer Projects streamlined workflows at PK Solutions. Prasad and David now collaborate efficiently, eliminating delays and miscommunication. The automation of data transfer ensures faster project kick-offs and improved customer satisfaction.

Regards ,

entry.title: SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud_FSCM_credit limit_custom approval workflow
entry.content:
Hello Experts, 

We have a requirement to create custom workflow approval workflow on value based to two level for approval. 

Requirement : 

To create custom approval workflow when the credit limit exceeds INR 5000000 for 7 days & if the customer credit limit exceeds the above value then approval should be triggered to 2 levels.

Please advise whether this custom approval workflow in FSCM is possible or not in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud.

Thank you

kind regards

Thangaraj Thomas

entry.title: Re: SAP RAP Managed (without draft) Popup Confirmation Message with ok & cancel button
entry.content: We helped you to accept the answer, since there were no updates after 7 days. You can unaccept it anytime if the answer provided was not helpful enough and you have further questions.

entry.title: Re: Automating SAP DSP Task Chains with CLI Wrapping: FastAPI Integration with SAP Analytics Cloud
entry.content:
Hi @Martin_Kuma ,

yes exactly, based on the setup of the OAuth Client and the refresh token you need to update the secrets manually. I did not find any other issues so far.

Regards

Christian

entry.title: Re: Speech To Text in SAP Mobile Start on iOS
entry.content:
Alright so i've found a solution, but i dont really know what the consequences of these solutions will be long term: 
There is a option to disable the new Sap Mobile Start Browser. As i said i dont know what else might be breaking with that change, but at least this old browsers allows managing Microphone permissions. 

I will not close this case, since this seems less like a fix but more like a temporary fix

entry.title: Re: FocusBuild - TOCs not imported immediately
entry.content:
Hi Andre,

If you are using solman 7.2, then you could run tcode: sm34>AIC_SETTINGS2->find your transaction type, like S1MJ or Z1MJ? In the left side bar, click 'Assign actions'->find the 'to be tested' status E0004->There check if action COPY_ALL_ENH used or only COPY_ALL used?

Action COPY_ALL_ENH will import ToC automatically when you move to status to be tested. While COPY_ALL will only release the ToC, you need to manually import the ToC via task list.

If you use COPY_ALL_ENH already, but the ToC is still not imported yet, then please open a support ticket to SAP under component SV-SMG-OST-FB-CM, describe your issue in detail, it's better to attach a document with screenshots to show your issues. And open your system connections for checking further.

BR,

Shawn

entry.title: Re: SAP TRM: Separation of loan principal and interest pa...
entry.content:
Hi, Tobias,

The flows from principal and interest are using different flow types and update types, and should not be posted into same GL Account.

If this is not the case in your tenant, I would suggest you to raise an OSS message so that our colleague could further check the configuration in your system to guide you to achieve that.

Would it be ok for you?

Kind regards

entry.title: Re: Generate PDF forms in S/4 HANA Public Cloud (Developer extensibility) using BTP Adobe Forms serv
entry.content:
Hi @diego_valentino

In the constructor method of the class ZCL_QM_TEMPLATE_UTIL, we are getting the bearer token using token service which is passed initially in “GET_TEMPLATE” method and then we are using the same bearer token in method “GET_TEMPLATE_BY_NAME” to get the template. Here is the attached screenshot for your reference.

entry.title: Re: SAP MDG: How to make grey / non-editable any fields o...
entry.content:
Hi Sandeep,

There are multiple ways to do it such as using BADI, Handler class and Feeder class. However, the best way is to use BADI 'USMD_ACC_FLD_PROP_CUST_DEP_SET' in which there is method 'MODIFY_FLD_PROP_ATTR' where you will get some import parameters using which you can write some condition such as based on entity type or change request type, and then change the values in changing parameter 'CT_FLD_PROP' for that particular field.

entry.title: Re: CA Introscope
entry.content:
Dear Customer, 

There can be many reasons why the EM in the Solution Manager configuration step is offline. Most of the time it's suggested to troubleshoot as KBA 2979157 - Introscope Enterprise Manager Step is offline / Gray / Undefined due to user authentication - SAP Solution Manager 7.2. Please first make sure that EM is online at the OS level. The web view is accessible in the browser. Then check the Solution Manager JAVA defaultTarce for more detailed reasons.

Best regards,
Vera Jiang

entry.title: Re: Automating SAP DSP Task Chains with CLI Wrapping: FastAPI Integration with SAP Analytics Cloud
entry.content:
Hi @CasCriel ,

yes I think this could be possible, but would need to be tested. Do you need to fetch an CSRF Toke  like it is necessary in the SCIM API (https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_DATASPHERE/9f804b8efa8043539289f42f372c4862/1ca8c4a9467f43df9ae6d4ed3734f05a.html#obtain-a-csrf-token)? I think something like this could make it challenging.

Regards

Christian

entry.title: Re: SAP Cloud SDK (Javascript) does not generate bound ac...
entry.content:
Hi Daniel,

I am facing the same problem but according to this issue https://github.com/SAP/cloud-sdk-js/issues/707 it is currently not supported to generate a corresponding OData client since it would be much effort to implement and they are waiting for "bigger demand".

I am going to comment on the issue hoping they now have capacities to implement it. If the topic is still relevant for you maybe you can leave a comment there as well to generate more demand.

Best regards,
Andre

entry.title: Re: Automating SAP DSP Task Chains with CLI Wrapping: FastAPI Integration with SAP Analytics Cloud
entry.content:
Hi @JulianJuraske,

I agree, it would be great to have an official solution provided.

The CLI on the local machine is necessary for the initial setup and for retrieving the secrets.json when the refresh token expires. In my case, the are two separate BTP accounts.

Thank you for pointing out the blog. I believe this approach is quite similar to what @CasCriel  mentioned, and it seems like a potential solution as well!

Regards

Christian

entry.title: Re: Segregation of inventory on the basis of budget consumed
entry.content:
Hello @Sermad 

You can use Project Stock special stock to manage your own resources projects and consumer financed project separately. 

Alternatively Split Valuation can be used to split your inventory into two segments: one for your own projects, the other for consumer financed ones. Also see Split Valuation in Inventory Management

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

entry.title: Re: Planning of materials provided to Subcon Vendor at MR...
entry.content: A Bill of Materials (BOM) is a structured list of Components along with it's quantities that are required to manufacture a Header Material (FG / SFG).

entry.title: Re: SAP group Reporting drill down to Manager journal ent...
entry.content:
Possible that your screen-shot contains data records from currency translation and not the ones from the release task. Note that both are recorded on the same posting level (blank) / document type (0F).

Try to filter to record type = U or currency translation indicator = 0 and make sure the data records displayed are from the release task.

If there still is no navigation link, you should create a customer message.

entry.title: Re: Optimizing Data Load for Large Replication Flow Object
entry.content:
Hi,

First thing is to make sure your S/4HANA has all the latest Replication Flow related notes implemented.

Use program CNV_NOTE_ANALYZER_DI to identify them (https://me.sap.com/notes/3016862).

Regards,

Clément

entry.title: Re: ESPM Webshop on light weight HTML5 applications
entry.content: This ESPM webshop has been down for quite some time now, unfortunately!!

entry.title: Re: 🇩🇪 Getting started with Generative AI Hub on SAP AI Core
entry.content: Quick question. I may have missed it, but what are the tech requirements? I image: bring your own laptop with VS Code installed and SAP AI Core and/or a BTP trial account?

entry.title: Re: 🇩🇪 Getting started with Generative AI Hub on SAP AI Core
entry.content:
You can find the content and prerequisites here:

https://github.com/SAP-samples/generative-ai-codejam

entry.title: Re: Sales Pricing analysis Fiori App
entry.content: Hello, thank you for your reply. The screenshots you mentioned above are VA02 Tcode Fiori app but not native Fiori App(F3893).

entry.title: Re: Event Based Architecture - An Overview
entry.content:
Hi Thomas,

thanks for the introductions into the concept of an event mesh, which gives organisations amazing flexibility to connect applications worldwide in realtime.

Just keep in mind that the "SAP Event Mesh" - the product - does not in fact provide the capability on an event mesh, since it is a single (shared) event broker. The event mesh capability comes with the newer and appropriately named "SAP Integration Suite, advanced event mesh". 

Dirk

entry.title: Re: IAS as central user management system
entry.content: Hi @BhagyaVenkatesha, Thank you very much for the response. Does this mean that if I have Azure as the IdP, I cannot use IAS as the central user management system? Many Thanks

entry.title: Re: 🟡 Techtoberfest 2024
Re: Why I love SAP and Blockchain Databases and why you should too 🚀
entry.content:
07/10/2024

hot off the presses, the latest blog is out:

Cyber Security Protection for S/4HANA Backups with Enterprise Blockchain and SAP BTP Kyma 🚀

the next one, 

Publish & Protect: with SAP Advanced Event Mesh & Enterprise Blockchain & SAP BTP Kyma 🚀

is in preparation

Andy.

entry.title: Re: Best Practices for Data Migration in a Carve-Out from S/4HANA On-Prem to S/4HANA Cloud
entry.content:
Hi Yannick,

To make this transitions more easy we've created a set of extraction programs that pulls the data out of ECC into the Excel/XML format to is required by the data migration cockpit (DMC). These programs allow selection of the data to overcome the problem of moving all ancient data and only select the relevant, active data. 

Because the output can be uploaded into DMC without any manual adjustments this ensures a fast and repeatable data migration process.

let me know if you need more info on this,

best regards,

Jeroen

entry.title: Re: Using table alias in order by of window expressions - ABAP open SQL
entry.content:
Hi  saurabh__khare,

Facing same issue. By any chance, Have you found any solution for this issue? I will also post if I found one. TIA.

entry.title: Vector Indexing for Retrieval-Augmented Generation with Databricks, SAP HANA, and Generative AI Hub
entry.content:
Introduction

In the rapidly evolving landscape of artificial intelligence, the combination of vector indexing and retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) has emerged as a powerful approach for enhancing the accuracy and relevance of generated content. By leveraging the capabilities of Databricks, SAP HANA Database, and tools for Generative AI like the Generative AI Hub SDK and LangChain, we can create a robust framework for efficient and scalable vector indexing.
In my previous blog post, we saw how LangChain could be used with SAP HANA for vector indexing and RAG. In this blog post, we will explore the step-by-step process of setting up a vector indexing pipeline using Databricks for data processing, SAP HANA as the vector database, and the Generative AI Hub SDK alongside LangChain for seamless integration. At each step, code examples for a Databricks notebook will be provided. Whether you're a data scientist, AI researcher, or a developer looking to enhance your RAG workflows, this guide will provide you with the insights and practical knowledge needed to get started. Let's dive into the intricacies of this powerful combination and unlock new potentials for your AI-driven projects.

Setting Up Our Environment

Databricks clusters come with a good number of pre-installed packages for data science, data engineering and AI purposes, so tools like Spark and LangChain are already available by default. However, there are some packages that we will need for the example pipeline that we will be building:
  • html2text: For converting text from HTML to Markdown format
  • generative-ai-hub-sdk: For working with Generative AI models in the Generative AI Hub
  • hdbcli: For connection to a HANA Database

The following code snippet could be executed in Databricks to install these packages:

 

%pip install html2text "generative-ai-hub-sdk[all]" hdbcli

dbutils.library.restartPython()

 

 

Note: While generative-ai-hub-sdk and hdbcli are required for the vector indexing process, html2text is only required for this example. Your use cases might not necessarily require html2text, but might require other packages instead, so you should adjust the pip install command accordingly to fit your use cases.

Building Our Vector Indexing Pipeline

Building a vector indexing pipeline is essentially a data engineering task, and as such, we could split the pipeline processes into the Extract, Transform, and Load (ETL) steps.

Extract

The extract step involves ingesting raw data from one or more data sources. For our example, our raw data are HTML files exported from an Atlassian Confluence wiki space, where each HTML file contains the contents of a single wiki page. These files are stored in a container within an Azure Data Lake Service (ADLS) account, and can be read using PySpark as shown:

 

from pyspark.sql.functions import expr

HTML_FILEPATHS_GLOB_PATTERN = "abfss://aitm@coredatalaketestint.dfs.core.windows.net/sandbox/example_wikis/*"

html_binary_spark_df = (
    spark
    .read
    .format("binaryFile")
    .options(pathGlobFilter="*.html")
    .load(HTML_FILEPATHS_GLOB_PATTERN)
    .withColumn("content", expr("CAST(content AS STRING)"))
)

 

 

If the extract step goes well, we should get a PySpark DataFrame containing these columns:
  • path: the full paths to the HTML files, which look something like abfss://aitm@coredatalaketestint.dfs.core.windows.net/sandbox/example_wikis/GAI01.03.10-RAGe_4337208805.html
  • modificationTime: timestamp values indicating when the files were last modified
  • length: length of the file contents
  • content: decoded HTML contents of the files

 

Note:
  • The snippet above shows how we can ingest data that exists as raw binary files in ADLS, but your use cases might involve data in different formats (e.g. CSV, Parquet) or from different sources (e.g. database, API), so you will need to adjust your ingestion logic accordingly. The documentation for working with inputs and outputs using PySpark is a good place to figure out how you could use Spark for ingesting your data.
  • In this example, HTML_FILEPATHS_GLOB_PATTERN points to a container in ADLS where the raw data is stored, with aitm being the container name, coredatalaketestint.dfs.core.windows.net being the address of the ADLS instance, and sandbox/example_wikis/ being the folder within the aitm container where the data is stored. For different cloud platforms (GCP, AWS etc), the URI would be different, so it is advised to check the relevant documentations from Spark or the respective cloud platforms.

Transform

After we have ingested our data, the next step is to clean and preprocess our data into a suitable format for vector indexing. For this example, we will perform the following to preprocess our data:
  • Converting the contents from HTML to Markdown: Markdown is syntactically simpler compared to HTML, which makes it more understandable to Large Language Models (LLMs), thus we do this conversion
  • Removing unnecessary contents: We will remove headers and footers in page contents to reduce noise in the data, which could help improve the retrieval performance of a RAG application. We also remove authors from the contents for privacy reasons.
  • Getting the base file names: This is an optional step to get the base file names from the path values in the path column, for recording as metadata during the indexing step later.
These smaller steps can be done as shown:

 

import re
import html2text
from pyspark.sql.functions import udf, col
from pyspark.sql.types import StringType

AUTHORS_REGEX_PATTERN = re.compile(r"Created\sby.*last\smodified.*[A-Z][a-z]{2}\s\d{2}\,\s\d{4}")

convert_html_to_markdown = udf(html2text.html2text, StringType())

@udf(StringType())
def strip_header_and_footer(content: str) -> str:
    return content[53:-100]

@udf(StringType())
def remove_authors(content: str) -> str:
    return AUTHORS_REGEX_PATTERN.sub("", content)

@udf(StringType())
def get_filename(path: str) -> str:
    return path.split("/")[-1]

wiki_texts_spark_df = (
    html_binary_spark_df
    .withColumn("content", convert_html_to_markdown(col("content")))
    .withColumn("content", strip_header_and_footer(col("content")))
    .withColumn("content", remove_authors(col("content")))
    .withColumn("filename", get_filename(col("path")))
    .selectExpr("filename", "content")
)

 

 

If the cleaning and preprocessing steps go well, we should get a different PySpark DataFrame containing these columns:
  • filename: the base name of the HTML files, which look something like GAI01.03.10-RAGe_4337208805.html
  • content: the cleaned contents of the wiki pages in Markdown format

 

Note: Spark user-defined functions (UDFs) can be used to apply preprocessing steps to the columns of a PySpark DataFrame in a simple and clean fashion. More information about PySpark UDFs can be found in the PySpark documentation.
After cleaning our data, we will still need to split our wiki contents into chunks. This is especially important if we are working with LLMs that have lower token limits. We also typically want to have smaller chunks of text to reduce the risk of encountering the lost-in-the-middle problem during the RAG process.
For our example, we use LangChain to load the PySpark DataFrame and perform the chunking of wiki contents:

 

from langchain_community.document_loaders import PySparkDataFrameLoader
from langchain_text_splitters import RecursiveCharacterTextSplitter

full_contents_loader = PySparkDataFrameLoader(spark_session=spark, df=wiki_texts_spark_df, page_content_column="content")
documents_to_split = full_contents_loader.load()

document_splitter = RecursiveCharacterTextSplitter.from_tiktoken_encoder(
    model_name="gpt-4",
    chunk_size=200,
    chunk_overlap=20
)

document_chunks = document_splitter.split_documents(documents_to_split)

 

 

Note: While we use the RecursiveCharacterTextSplitter class from LangChain with chunk_size of 200 and chunk_overlap of 20 as an example, other chunking strategies might work better for your own use cases. Therefore, some exploration should be done to determine chunking strategies that work better for your use cases. For a starting place on chunking strategy, you can refer to this Medium post.

Load

Now that we have cleaned and preprocessed our data, we are ready for the indexing step itself.
As we are using an embedding model from the Generative AI Hub as well as the HANA Database for the vector indexing step, we need to load the secrets for these services. For this example, these secrets have been stored as Databricks secrets which can be loaded as shown:

 

import json

SECRET_SCOPE = "TEST_AITM_SCOPE"
gen_ai_hub_service_key = json.loads(dbutils.secrets.get(scope=SECRET_SCOPE, key="EXAMPLE_GENAI_HUB_SERVICE_KEY"))
hana_secrets = json.loads(dbutils.secrets.get(scope=SECRET_SCOPE, key="EXAMPLE_HANA_VECTOR_SECRETS"))

 

After loading the secrets, we want to initialize the client objects for Generative AI Hub and HANA Database respectively as shown:

 

# HANA DB
from hdbcli import dbapi

hana_conn = dbapi.connect(
    address=hana_secrets["host"],
    port=hana_secrets["port"],
    user=hana_secrets["user"],
    password=hana_secrets["password"],
    autocommit=True,
    sslTrustStore=hana_secrets["certificate"],
)


# GenAI Hub
import os
from gen_ai_hub.proxy.core.proxy_clients import get_proxy_client

os.environ["AICORE_AUTH_URL"] = gen_ai_hub_service_key["url"]
os.environ["AICORE_CLIENT_ID"] = gen_ai_hub_service_key["clientid"]
os.environ["AICORE_CLIENT_SECRET"] = gen_ai_hub_service_key["clientsecret"]
os.environ["AICORE_RESOURCE_GROUP"] = gen_ai_hub_service_key["appname"].split("!")[0]
os.environ["AICORE_BASE_URL"] = f"{gen_ai_hub_service_key['serviceurls']['AI_API_URL']}/v2"

proxy_client = get_proxy_client("gen-ai-hub")

 

We then want to initialize the LangChain objects for working with our embedding model and HANA DB:

 

from gen_ai_hub.proxy.langchain.init_models import init_embedding_model
from langchain_community.vectorstores import HanaDB

embeddings = init_embedding_model("text-embedding-ada-002", proxy_client=proxy_client)
hana_vectordb = HanaDB(embedding=embeddings, connection=hana_conn, table_name="DATABRICKS_HANA_EXAMPLE_VECTORSTORE")

 

Note: My previous blog post gives more explanation of this code snippet, so you may want to check it out.

The last bit of the indexing process is a simple method call to write the chunks to HANA DB. LangChain will take care of the embedding of chunks and writing to HANA under the hood.

 

hana_vectordb.add_documents(document_chunks)

 

Optionally, we can run the following code snippet to test the retrieval of document chunks, as a sanity check:

 

hana_vector_retriever = hana_vectordb.as_retriever()

hana_vector_retriever.get_relevant_documents("What is RAG?")

 

 

What Next?

Now that we have our pipeline notebook, the typical next step would be to make our pipeline scalable by creating a Databricks job that runs that notebook. Configurations such as trigger schedules or notifications depend on the use case and can be set in Databricks accordingly. More information can be found in the Databricks documentation for jobs.
The vectors that are indexed can be consumed by a Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) application to enhance the relevance and accuracy of generated content. For an example of how to consume these vectors using LangChain, refer to my previous blog post where I demonstrate the integration and utilization of these vectors in a RAG workflow.

Conclusion

In this blog post, we've delved into the intricate process of setting up a vector indexing pipeline using Databricks, SAP HANA, and Generative AI tools like the Generative AI Hub SDK and LangChain. By carefully walking through each step—from data extraction and transformation to loading and indexing—we've demonstrated how to build a scalable and efficient pipeline that significantly enhances the accuracy and relevance of AI-generated content. This powerful combination of technologies not only streamlines your RAG workflows but also opens new avenues for innovation in AI-driven projects.  Feel free to leave your comments and share your thoughts or questions about this blog post.
Happy coding and may your AI projects reach new heights of excellence! 🥂

entry.title: Re: Formatted Search PO Screen
entry.content:
Hi You could try to test your FMS

Open the form and then select the query through the menu. the palceholder will be filled and you could see what happen.

regards Lothar

entry.title: Re: Re-creating a Custom Field with the Same Name After D...
entry.content: Thank you for the information. I would like to know more about on-premise. Can it be confirmed that the note you provided also applies to on-premise environments?

entry.title: Re: solr admin page connection refused after upgrading to...
entry.content:
Thank you. That worked for me.

I actually had to edit the file under the following path:

sap-commerce\core-customize\hybris\bin\modules\search-and-navigation\solrserver\resources\solr\9.5\server\bin\solr.in.sh

entry.title: Re: 🟡 Techtoberfest 2024
entry.content: Just my humble setup  #techtoberfest
